english: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='20'> 4 E 20 This tower once served as a meeting place where those brave souls who achieved safe passage to Skyrim would find loved ones, and leave notice for others who could not be found. Let it stand in honor of those who had the strength and spirit to accept Skyrim's Offer "untithed to any thane or hold, and self-governed, with free worship, with no compensation to Skyrim or the Empire except as writ in the Armistice of old wheresoever those might still apply, and henceforth let no Man or Mer say that the Sons and Daughters of Kyne are without mercy or honor." We, the Jarls of Skyrim, hereby decree this site as monument to the struggle of those who fled their native home of Morrowind in the time following the Red Year. </font>
chinese: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='20'> 4E20年 此塔楼曾是那些抵达天际的勇敢灵魂聚会的场所，在这里他们可以找到自己的亲人，并为那些无法找到的人留下提示。 让它为那些有力量和精神接受天际提议者的荣誉而耸立吧，“不向任何武卫或领地缴纳赋税，自信自治，不在旧有《停战协议》这一仍适用的条约以外对天际或帝国做出任何赔偿，从今往后，任何人与莫都不能说凯娜的子女是没有怜悯与荣誉的。” 我等天际领主在此宣告，此地为红色之年后从晨风背井离乡的奋斗者的纪念碑。 </font>

english: I'm sick of repeating myself. Hand this to the man replacing you between shifts, and ensure he reads it before you leave the post. I am not to be disturbed. Permit no one in my chamber, ever. Certain people may come looking for me. I want those people dead. You'll be back to raiding pig farms tomorrow if you foul this up.

English: The Dowry

english:
[pagebreak]

The Waters of Oblivion

[pagebreak]

hundred and twenty numbered ages in the void that fated folk had grown deep-schooled in evil. Then the Bright Gods resolved to punish those faithless spirits, and shatter the unruly caitiffs, those huge, unholy scathers, loathsome to the Light. They repented exceedingly that they had gazed upon Oblivion, and seen there the first of dark kin, and welcomed them as brothers and sisters. [pagebreak] he Principalities of Victory beheld how great was the wickedness of the wayward spirits, and saw that they were bold in sin and full of wiles. They resolved then to chasten the tribes of daedra, and smite darkkind with hammer and hand. [pagebreak] ut ever shall Darkness contest the Light, and great were the Powers that breathed the void and laid waste upon one another, and no oath might bind them, so deep were they in envy and perfidy. For once the portals are opened, who shall shut them upon the rising tide?
chinese:
[pagebreak]

湮灭之水

[pagebreak]

虚空已过百二十载，命定之人已深陷邪恶。辉耀诸神决心惩罚堕落之灵，摧毁不端可鄙、带来伤痛、亵渎光明之人。他们因凝望湮灭而无尽懊悔，暗族现身，给予如兄弟姐妹般的欢迎。 [pagebreak] 胜利诸王洞察反复无常之灵的伟力，目睹其犯罪胆大妄为，诡谋频出。遂决议惩戒迪德拉，猛击暗族。 [pagebreak] 但黑暗将永与光明竞逐，而巨大的力量将吞噬虚空、彼此蹂躏，它们不为誓言所缚，深陷嫉妒与背叛。有朝一日湮灭之门开启，谁能阻挡这股洪流？

english:
A mighty lord deserves a mighty offering
But he who fell on this spot asked to be remembered humbly
We who served at his hand, however, do not want his brilliance to be forgotten
Thus, to honor him, one needs only to look to the simple, glowing fungus on these cave walls
For no matter how mighty or humble one may be in life
We all return to the same ground from which this mushroom blooms
chinese:
强大的君主本应供奉盛大的祭品，
但殒没于此之人要求大家谦卑地铭记他。
然而我等曾效忠于他之人不愿他的辉煌被人遗忘，
因此，为了纪念他，人们只需看着洞穴壁上那些普普通通的发光真菌。
无论一个人生前强大抑或卑微，
我们最终都将回归这蘑菇生长的地方。

agentText:
Addon: Skyrim
EDID: Favor255QuestReturnTopic
Record: DIAL:FULL
SID: 00939D
English: I've helped your people.
Chinese: 我帮助过您的人民了。

english: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> The eyeless creatures took us in our sleep. I don't know what happened to the Khajiit brothers, we never saw them in the cell. I managed to pick the lock and we made a break for it, but got split up. Sulla yelled something about not leaving without finding what he came here for and Umana chased after him. Yag and I tried for the top of the cave shaft, but one of the ramps was broken. Without a hesitation, she grabbed me by the scruff of my tunic, threw me atop the ledge and told me to run. And I did. I didn't even look back. I just ran like a coward. I could hear her fighting them and I just had to get away. I didn't even notice the arrow in my shoulder till I hid here. [pagebreak] Those metal creatures are still all around me and I'm too terrified to even move. Eight Divines, please just take me now. </font>
chinese: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> 那些不长眼的生物在我们睡觉时把我们抓了。不知道卡吉特那哥俩怎么样了，没在牢房看见他们。我把锁撬开了。虽然我们逃出来，但却分开了。苏拉大喊着不找到目标就不离开，乌马娜去追他了。 我和雅格试着往洞顶爬，但其中一个坡道坏了。她毫不犹豫地抓起我的衣领，把我扔到了岩架上，让我赶紧跑。 所以我跑了。 我连回头看一眼的勇气都没有，就这么像个懦夫一样逃跑了。 我听到她在后面搏斗的声音，却不得不离开。 我躲到这里之前都没注意到肩膀上中箭了。 [pagebreak] 那些金属生物还在我周围，吓得我动都不敢动。 八圣灵啊，快带我走吧。 </font>
englishNormalized: <font face='$handwrittenfont'><font size='18'> the eyeless creatures took us in our sleep. i don't know what happened to the khajiit brothers, we never saw them in the cell. i managed to pick the lock and we made a break for it, but got split up. sulla yelled something about not leaving without finding what he came here for and umana chased after him. yag and i tried for the top of the cave shaft, but one of the ramps was broken. without a hesitation, she grabbed me by the scruff of my tunic, threw me atop the ledge and told me to run. and i did. i didn't even look back. i just ran like a coward. i could hear her fighting them and i just had to get away. i didn't even notice the arrow in my shoulder till i hid here. [pagebreak] those metal creatures are still all around me and i'm too terrified to even move. eight divines, please just take me now. </font>

English: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> The eyeless creatures took us in our sleep. I don't know what happened to the Khajiit brothers, we never saw them in the cell. I managed to pick the lock and we made a break for it, but got split up. Sulla yelled something about not leaving without finding what he came here for and Umana chased after him. Yag and I tried for the top of the cave shaft, but one of the ramps was broken. Without a hesitation, she grabbed me by the scruff of my tunic, threw me atop the ledge and told me to run. And I did. I didn't even look back. I just ran like a coward. I could hear her fighting them and I just had to get away. I didn't even notice the arrow in my shoulder till I hid here. english: The missing students were found in the Midden this morning. Dead, as expected. None of us bothered keeping a detect life charm for the search at this point. The bodies were found together, each suffering the same deformities; peeled and bubbling skin on the arms and face. Conjurer's burn, as it's commonly referred to around the College. There's little doubt they were attempting a summoning ritual well beyond their capabilities. The relic nearby put any doubt in this theory to rest. I admit that I've never seen one like it - a large, segmented sculpture of a gauntlet, the Daedric sigil 'O' emblazoned upon the palm. Attempts to move it were in vain. I must show it to Archmage Sedoth during his upcoming visit. Perhaps he will know more. While we couldn't move the relic, I was able to pry four "rings" from it. I'm sure there's a connection between them and the ritual the students were attempting. I'll store these in the Arcanaeum until we can consult with a conjuration master to learn more.
chinese: 失踪的学生已在秽道发现。如预期一样，全部死亡。我们没有再费心去保持探测生命的魔法了。 这些尸体毫无疑问是一同被发现的，每个都有同样的畸形现象：手臂和脸部的皮肤被剥去或起泡。这是“召唤师灼烧”，学院里很常见，他们毫无疑问是尝试了一种超过自己能力范围的召唤仪式。 附近的遗物打消了所有疑虑。我从未见过如此巨大、分段的护手雕塑，手掌心上还雕刻着迪德拉文的“O” 字纹样。试图移动无果。我必须在大法师瑟多斯到来的时候展示给他。也许他会知道更多。 虽然我们无法移动该遗物，但我从里面撬出四个“指环”。相信它们与学徒们尝试的仪式有一定联系。 我要把这些东西放在秘藏馆，直到我们从召唤大师处了解更多。

agentText:
Addon: Skyrim EDID: dunMiddenInvestigationBook Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 0094EF English:

The missing students were found in the Midden this morning. Dead, as expected. None of us bothered keeping a detect life charm for the search at this point. The bodies were found together, each suffering the same deformities; peeled and bubbling skin on the arms and face. Conjurer's burn, as it's commonly referred to around the College. There's little doubt they were attempting a summoning ritual well beyond their capabilities. The relic nearby put any doubt in this theory to rest. I admit that I've never seen one like it - a large, segmented sculpture of a gauntlet, the Daedric sigil 'O' emblazoned upon the palm. Attempts to move it were in vain. I must show it to Archmage Sedoth during his upcoming visit. Perhaps he will know more. While we couldn't move the relic, I was able to pry four "rings" from it. I'm sure there's a connection between them and the ritual the students were attempting. I'll store these in the Arcanaeum until we can consult with a conjuration master to learn more. Chinese:

失踪的学生已在秽道发现。如预期一样，全部死亡。我们没有再费心去保持探测生命的魔法了。 这些尸体毫无疑问是一同被发现的，每个都有同样的畸形现象：手臂和脸部的皮肤被剥去或起泡。这是“召唤师灼烧”，学院里很常见，他们毫无疑问是尝试了一种超过自己能力范围的召唤仪式。 附近的遗物打消了所有疑虑。我从未见过如此巨大、分段的护手雕塑，手掌心上还雕刻着迪德拉文的“O” 字纹样。试图移动无果。我必须在大法师瑟多斯到来的时候展示给他。也许他会知道更多。 虽然我们无法移动该遗物，但我从里面撬出四个“指环”。相信它们与学徒们尝试的仪式有一定联系。 我要把这些东西放在秘藏馆，直到我们从召唤大师处了解更多。

english:
[pagebreak]

A Dance in Fire Chapter 4 by Waughin Jarth

[pagebreak]

ighteen Bosmeri and one Cyrodilic former senior clerk for an Imperial building commission trudged through the jungle westward from the Xylo River to the ancient village of Vindisi. For Decumus Scotti, the jungle was hostile, unfamiliar ground. The enormous vermiculated trees filled the bright morning with darkness, and resembled nothing so much as grasping claws, bent on impeding their progress. Even the fronds of the low plants quivered with malevolent energy. What was worse, he was not alone in his anxiety. His fellow travelers, the natives who had survived the Khajiit attacks on the villages of Grenos and Athay, wore faces of undisguised fear. There was something sentient in the jungle, and not merely the mad but benevolent indigenous spirits. In his peripheral vision, Scotti could see the shadows of the Khajiiti following the refugees, leaping from tree to tree. When he turned to face them, the lithe forms vanished into the gloom as if they had never been there. But he knew he had seen them. And the Bosmeri saw them too, and quickened their pace. After eighteen hours, bitten raw by insects, scratched by a thousand thorns, they emerged into a valley clearing. It was night, but a row of blazing torches greeted them, illuminating the leather-wrought tents and jumbled stones of the hamlet of Vindisi. At the end of the valley, the torches marked a sacred site, a gnarled bower of trees pressed closed together to form a temple. Wordlessly, the Bosmeri walked the torch arcade toward the trees. Scotti followed them. When they reached the solid mass of living wood with only one gaping portal, Scotti could see a dim blue light glowing within. A low sonorous moan from a hundred voices echoed within. The Bosmeri maiden he had been following held out her hand, stopping him. "You do not understand, but no outsider, not even a friend may enter," she said. "This is a holy place." Scotti nodded, and watched the refugees march into the temple, heads bowed. Their voices joined with the ones within. When the last wood elf had gone inside, Scotti turned his attention back to the village. There must be food to be had somewhere. A tendril of smoke and a faint whiff of roasting venison beyond the torchlight led him. They were five Cyrodiils, two Bretons, and a Nord, the group gathered around a campfire of glowing white stones, pulling steaming strips of meat from the cadaver of a great stag. At Scotti's approach, they rose up, all but the Nord who was distracted by his hunk of animal flesh. "Good evening, sorry to interrupt, but I was wondering if I might have a little something to eat. I'm afraid I'm rather hungry, after walking all day with some refugees from Grenos and Athay." They bade him to sit down and eat, and introduced themselves. "So the war's back on, it seems," said Scotti amiably. "Best thing for these effete do-nothings," replied the Nord in between bites. "I've never seen such a lazy culture. Now they've got the Khajiiti striking them on land, and the high elves at sea. If there's any province that deserves a little distress, it's damnable Valenwood." "I don't see how they're so offensive to you," laughed one of the Bretons. "They're congenital thieves, even worse than the Khajiiti because they are so blessed meek in their aggression," the Nord spat out a gob of fat which sizzled on the hot stones of the fire. "They spread their forests into territory that doesn't belong to them, slowly infiltrating their neighbors, and they're puzzled when Elsweyr shoves back at them. They're all villains of the worst order." "What are you doing here?" asked Scotti. "I'm a diplomat from the court of Jehenna," muttered the Nord, returning to his food. "What about you, what are you doing here?" asked one of the Cyrodiils. "I work for Lord Atrius's building commission in the Imperial City," said Scotti. "One of my former colleagues suggested that I come down to Valenwood. He said the war was over, and I could contract a great deal of business for my firm rebuilding what was lost. One disaster after another, and I've lost all my money, I'm in the middle of a rekindling of war, and I cannot find my former colleague." "Your former colleague," murmured another of the Cyrodiils, who had introduced himself as Reglius. "He wasn't by any chance named Liodes Jurus, was he?" "You know him?" "He lured me down to Valenwood in nearly the exact same circumstances," smiled Reglius, grimly. "I worked for your employer's competitor, Lord Vanech's men, where Liodes Jurus also formerly worked. He wrote to me, asking that I represent an Imperial building commission and contract some post-war construction. I had just been released from my employment, and I thought that if I brought some new business, I could have my job back. Jurus and I met in Athay, and he said he was going to arrange a very lucrative meeting with the Silvenar." Scotti was stunned: "Where is he now?" "I'm no theologian, so I couldn't say," Reglius shrugged. "He's dead. When the Khajiiti attacked Athay, they began by torching the harbor where Jurus was readying his boat. Or, I should say, my boat since it was purchased with the gold I brought. By the time we were even aware of what was happening enough to flee, everything by the water was ash. The Khajiiti may be animals, but they know how to arrange an attack." "I think they followed us through the jungle to Vindisi," said Scotti nervously. "There was definitely a group of something jumping along the treetops." "Probably one of the monkey folk," snorted the Nord. "Nothing to be concerned about." "When we first came to Vindisi and the Bosmeri all entered that tree, they were furious, whispering something about unleashing an ancient terror on their enemies," the Breton shivered, remembering. "They've been there ever since, for over a day and a half now. If you want something to be afraid of, that's the direction to look." The other Breton, who was a representative of the Daggerfall Mages Guild, was staring off into the darkness while his fellow provincial spoke. "Maybe. But there's something in the jungle too, right on the edge of the village, looking in." "More refugees maybe?" asked Scotti, trying to keep the alarm out his voice. "Not unless they're traveling through the trees now," whispered the wizard. The Nord and one of the Cyrodiils grabbed a long tarp of wet leather and pulled it across the fire, instantly extinguishing it without so much as a sizzle. Now Scotti could see the intruders, their elliptical yellow eyes and long cruel blades catching the torchlight. He froze with fear, praying that he too was not so visible to them. He felt something bump against his back, and gasped. Reglius's voice hissed from up above: "Be quiet for Mara's sake and climb up here." Scotti grabbed hold of the knotted double-vine that hung down from a tall tree at the edge of the dead campfire. He scrambled up it as quickly as he could, holding his breath lest any grunt of exertion escape him. At the top of the vine, high above the village, was an abandoned nest from some great bird in a trident-shaped branch. As soon as Scotti had pulled himself into the soft, fragrant straw, Reglius pulled up the vine. No one else was there, and when Scotti looked down, he could see no one below. No one, that is except the Khajiiti, slowly moving toward the glow of the temple tree. "Thank you," whispered Scotti, deeply touched that a competitor had helped him. He turned away from the village, and saw that the tree's upper branches brushed against the mossy rock walls that surrounded the valley below. "How are you at climbing?" "You're mad," said Reglius under his breath. "We should stay here until they leave." "If they burn Vindisi like they did Athay and Grenos, we'll be dead sure as if we were on the ground," Scotti began the slow careful climb up the tree, testing each branch. "Can you see what they're doing?" "I can't really tell," Reglius stared down into the gloom. "They're at the front of the temple. I think they also have ... it looks like long ropes, trailing off behind them, off into the pass." Scotti crawled onto the strongest branch that pointed toward the wet, rocky face of the cliff. It was not a far jump at all. So close, in fact, that he could smell the moisture and feel the coolness of the stone. But it was a jump nevertheless, and in his history as a clerk, he had never before leapt from a tree a hundred feet off the ground to a sheer rock. He pictured in his mind's eye the shadows that had pursued him through the jungle from the heights above. How their legs coiled to spring, how their arms snapped forward in an elegant fluid motion to grasp. He leapt. His hands grappled for rock, but long thick cords of moss were more accessible. He held hard, but when he tried to plant his feet forward, they slipped up skyward. For a few seconds, he found himself upside down before he managed to pull himself into a more conventional position. There was a narrow outcropping jutting out of the cliff where he could stand and finally exhale. "Reglius. Reglius. Reglius," Scotti did not dare to call out. In a minute, there was a shaking of branches, and Lord Vanech's man emerged. First his satchel, then his head, then the rest of him. Scotti started to whisper something, but Reglius shook his head violently and pointed downward. One of the Khajiiti was at the base of the tree, peering at the remains of the campfire. Reglius awkwardly tried to balance himself on the branch, but as strong as it was it was exceedingly difficult with only one free hand. Scotti cupped his palms and then pointed at the satchel. It seemed to pain Reglius to let it out of his grasp, but he relented and tossed it to Scotti. There was a small, almost invisible hole in the bag, and when Scotti caught it, a single gold coin dropped out. It rang as it bounced against the rock wall on the descent, a high soft sound that seemed like the loudest alarm Scotti had ever heard. Then many things happened very quickly. The Cathay-Raht at the base of the tree looked up and gave a loud wail. The other Khajiiti followed in chorus, as the cat below crouched down and then sprung up into the lower branches. Reglius saw it below him, climbing up with impossible dexterity, and panicked. Even before he jumped, Scotti could tell that he was going to fall. With a cry, Reglius the Clerk plunged to the ground, breaking his neck on impact. A flash of white fire erupted from every crevice of the temple, and the moan of the Bosmeri prayer changed into something terrible and otherworldly. The climbing Cathay-Raht stopped and stared. "Keirgo," it gasped. "The Wild Hunt." It was as if a crack in reality had opened wide. A flood of horrific beasts, tentacled toads, insects of armor and spine, gelatinous serpents, vaporous beings with the face of gods, all poured forth from the great hollow tree, blind with fury. They tore the Khajiiti in front of the temple to pieces. All the other cats fled for the jungle, but as they did so, they began pulling on the ropes they carried. In a few seconds time, the entire village of Vindisi was boiling with the lunatic apparitions of the Wild Hunt. Over the babbling, barking, howling horde, Scotti heard the Cyrodiils in hiding cry out as they were devoured. The Nord too was found and eaten, and both Bretons. The wizard had turned himself invisible, but the swarm did not rely on their sight. The tree the Cathay-Raht was in began to sway and rock from the impossible violence beneath it. Scotti looked at the Khajiiti's fear-struck eyes, and held out one of the cords of moss. The cat's face showed its pitiful gratitude as it leapt for the vine. It didn't have time to entirely replace that expression when Scotti pulled back the cord, and watched it fall. The Hunt consumed it to the bone before it struck the ground. Scotti's own jump up to the next outcropping of rock was immeasurably more successful. From there, he pulled himself to the top of the cliff and was able to look down into the chaos that had been the village of Vindisi. The Hunt's mass had grown and began to spill out through the pass out of the valley, pursuing the fleeing Khajiiti. It was then that the madness truly began. In the moons' light, from Scotti's vantage, he could see where the Khajiiti had attached their ropes. With a thunderous boom, an avalanche of boulders poured over the pass. When the dust cleared, he saw that the valley had been sealed. The Wild Hunt had nowhere to turn but on itself. Scotti turned his head, unable to bear to look at the cannibalistic orgy. The night jungle stood before him, a web of wood. He slung Reglius's satchel over his shoulder, and entered.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

火中舞 卷四 瓦辛·贾斯 著

[pagebreak]

十八位波斯莫和一位曾于帝都建筑委员会就职的希罗帝尔文员，自木河向古老村落温蒂斯经，沿途穿林过木、步履沉重地跋涉了许久。对于戴奎莫斯·斯科提来说，这片丛林是一片饱含着敌意的陌生土地。有大量经虫蛀的繁茂树木掩住了晨曦，活像是攫取猎食的螯爪，一心阻碍着他们前进。甚至是低矮植物的叶片，也不怀好意地颤动不止。更糟的是，不单是斯科提一人焦虑难安。他的同行者们，这些在卡吉特对格仁诺斯和艾赛的进攻中幸存下来的本地居民，脸上纷纷不加掩饰地流露出恐惧之色。 不独是疯疯癫癫却心地善良的本地生灵，在树丛中尚有其它活物。在他的视野外围，斯科提能看见那些阴影时现，那属于尾随着难民而来的卡吉特，他们在树木上腾跃辗转。当他把脸转向他们时，那些矫健灵活的身影便消失在了黑暗中，仿佛从未存在过一般。可是他知道自己确实亲眼目击了他们。那些波斯莫也看见了，所以加快了步伐。 在十八小时后，被昆虫叮咬着伤口、被密密麻麻的千万道荆棘割伤的他们进入了从一片山谷空地。此时已经入夜，但有一排灼灼燃烧的火炬向着他们迎了过来，照亮了革质帐篷和温蒂斯村遍地随意堆砌的石块。在山谷尽头，火炬之光照耀着一处圣坛，森森树影中，无数枝条彼此相依，拱卫成一座神庙。无需多言，波斯莫们穿过设有火炬的廊道，向着树木走去。斯科提紧随其后。当他们到达这根茎坚固、撑天拄地的活体树木时，仅有一道大门洞开，斯科提能看见昏昧幽暗的蓝光在其中闪烁。百种声调齐鸣而起，汇合成一阵低沉浑厚的哀嚎，在洞中震起无数回音。那位始终带着他前行的波斯莫少女伸出手阻住了他。 “你不会明白的，但没有任何外人得以进入，甚至是朋友也不行。”她说。“这是一座圣地。” 斯科提略一颔首，看着难民们从神庙口低垂着头，鱼贯而入。他们的声音混入了洞中的回响。当最后一个木精灵进入到洞中，斯科提便将注意力转回了村庄。某些地方肯定还储存着食物。缭绕若丝的烟尘和些微烤鹿肉的气息越过火炬的光芒，引着他向气味发出的方向走去。 五名希罗帝尔人、两名布莱顿人和一名诺德人围着一簇营火聚集着。那火将白色的石头烧得发红。他们从一头巨大雄鹿尸体上撕扯下一条条散发着热气的肉片。在斯科提走近他们时，除了一位专心于自己那一大块兽肉的诺德人外，其他人都纷纷站了起来。 “晚上好，很抱歉冒昧打扰，但我想问问能否分我一点吃食。我和格仁诺斯与艾赛的难民们徒步走了整整一天，现在很饿了。” 他们招呼他坐下来用餐，并且各自做了自我介绍。 “看来战争是卷土重来了。”斯科提语气友善地发问道。 “现在这些废物蛋啥都别去惹，就谢天谢地了。”诺德人在咀嚼之余回应道，“我就没瞧见过这么懒散的文化。现在陆上有卡吉特，海上又有高精灵袭击他们。如果有哪个省份活该吃点小苦头，那铁定就是这天杀的威伦森林。” “我可没看见他们有冒犯过你。”布莱顿人中的一位大笑道。 “他们天生就是小贼，简直比卡吉特还坏，连侵略的时候也总是一副乖头乖脑的样子，”诺德人吐出一大块肥肉，那脂肪掉在了被火烤至高温的石头上，“滋滋”一声，发出一阵火焰燃烧油脂的轻响。“他们把自家的森林扩张到不属于他们的领土上，一点点渗透进他们的邻居，艾斯维尔反攻回来他们还摸不着头脑。他们都是些活在最差劲的社会里的混蛋。” “你在这里做什么呢？”斯科提发问。 “我是约赫纳宫廷派来的外交使臣。”诺德人嘀咕着说了一句，又回去吃他的食物。 “那你呢，你是来这干什么的？”其中一个希罗帝尔人问。 “我为帝都的阿特里乌斯大人的建筑委会工作。”斯科提说。“我的一位前同事建议我南下威伦森林。他说战争已经结束，于是我能够借助重修那些在战争中毁于一旦的建筑，为我的公司签下一份满是商机的大合同。灾厄接踵而至，我失去了所有钱财，还跑到了战火重燃的核心地区，而且，我还是没能找到我的前任同事。” “你的前任同事，”另一位自称雷格利乌斯的希罗帝尔人喃喃低语道，“他不会正好就叫利奥德斯·朱鲁斯吧，是他吗？” “你认识他？ “在我混得和你一模一样的时候，他把我引到南部的威伦森林。”雷格利乌斯冷冷地笑道，“我给你老板的竞争对头瓦内奇大人的手下工作，那也是利奥德斯·朱鲁斯曾经工作过的地方。他写信给我，让我代表帝国建筑委会，来签订某种战后重修的合约。我刚刚被解雇，还在想着如果我能带来些新的生意，我就能夺回我的工作。朱鲁斯同我在艾赛会面，并说他要安排一场和希尔维纳的商谈，这其中有大生意可做。” 斯科提诧异不已：“那他现在在哪？” “我不是神学家，所以我也不知道，”雷格利乌斯耸了耸肩。“他死了。在卡吉特们攻打艾赛的时候，他们开始焚烧港口，朱鲁斯那时候正准备着登上他的船离开。或者我应该说，我的船，因为那是花了我的金币买到的。甚至在我们充分意识到发生的事情从而逃离之前，水边的一切便焚作齑粉。卡吉特们可能是群畜生，但是他们很懂得怎么安排进攻。” “我认为他们已经尾随我们穿行过丛林，来到了温蒂斯，”斯科提紧张地陈述着，“在树梢之间肯定有什么生物在成群结队地跳跃。” “可能就是些猴人。”诺德人打了个喷嚏。“没啥好担心的。” “在我们刚到温蒂斯的时候，波斯莫全部进入了那棵树，他们躁动不安，低语着要向敌人释放一种古老的恐怖。”布莱顿人打着战回忆道。“他们就一直站在那，站了足足有一天半了。如果你想找点让你害怕的东西，那就往他们那个方向看一眼。” 另一位布莱顿人，即那位匕落法师公会的代表，在他的同乡发言时，目光茫然地盯着一片黑暗。“可能是吧。但是丛林里也有东西，就在村庄边缘，在向我们窥视。” “可能是其他的难民吧？”斯科提问道，视着让自己保持平静的口吻。 “除非他们现在正穿过树行进，”巫师悄声说着。诺德人和一名希罗帝尔人抓过湿皮革制成的长条油布，将它拉过来盖在了火上，火焰霎时熄灭，连一丝轻微的烧灼声也没有传出。现在斯科提可以看见那些不速之客，它们呈椭圆的黄色眼瞳，以及反射着火光、修长而嗜血的剑刃。他全身冻结在恐惧中，动弹不得，祈祷着自己在它们看来不要太过显眼。 他感到有什么东西碰了碰他的后背，不由倒吸一口凉气。 雷格利乌斯的声音嘶嘶地从上方传来：“看在玛拉的份上，给我安静点，从这爬上来。” 在业已熄灭的营火边缘，从一株高大树木上垂下一根由两道藤蔓绞缠而成的绳子，斯科提一把抓住绳子，全速地向上攀爬，小心地将呼吸放轻，以防有任何喘息声从他口鼻中逃逸出去。在藤蔓的最上端，有一座荒废的巨鸟巢穴坐落在如三叉戟一般的枝杈上，高悬于村庄上空。斯科提刚刚一头栽倒在散发着馥郁气息的柔软麦秆上，就看见雷格利乌斯将藤蔓拉上来收起。下方现已不余一人，而后，在斯科提俯瞰下视时，他也没有看到有任何人影。除了那些朝着那座树木神庙的光辉缓步移动的卡吉特。 “谢谢你，”斯科提耳语道，对这位曾经的竞争者予以他的帮助，内心触动不已。他从村庄方向转过头，看见树木上部的枝柯轻轻触着环抱谷底、生满苔藓的石壁。“你攀爬能力如何？” “你疯了吧，”雷格利乌斯按低了呼吸声说道。“我们就该在这待到他们离开。” “如果他们焚毁了温蒂斯，就像他们对艾赛和格仁诺斯所做的那样，我们下地就死定了。”斯科提开始小心缓慢地尝试爬上树，尝试着每一根枝条是否能供攀登。“你能看见他们在干什么吗？” “说实话，我看不太出来，”雷格利乌斯望向那一片幽暗中。“他们聚集在神庙前方。我想他们也带有……那看起来就像长绳，拖行在他们身后，一直延伸到路上。” 斯科提攀上了一根最为坚固粗硕的枝条，那枝条延伸向潮湿嶙峋的悬崖表面。想跳上崖岸，他所需要跃过的距离并不算远。实际上，这距离近得能让他嗅到丝丝水汽，切身感受到崖石间沁出的阴冷寒意。然而这依旧需要他跳出一步，而在他文员生涯中，他从未做过自去地百尺的树梢间腾跃上陡峭岩石这种事。在他的脑海中，浮现出了那些腾挪于树梢高处、穿林过叶追逐着他的身影。他想起了他们的腿部如何弯曲再发力跳起，他们的手臂如何前伸，优雅灵敏地抓稳树枝。然后，他一步跃起。 他的手努力够向岩石，然而更易于攫获的却是以长条带状生长出的茁茂苔藓。他紧紧抓握住苔藓须茎，然而当他尝试着将双足前蹬时，却不慎失足，两脚朝天。他发现自己上下颠倒，倒悬了几秒，直到他扯着苔藓，将自己拽到一个更习惯的姿势。一道狭窄的露头岩石自峭壁上支起，他得以立足其上，并在落脚后松了口气。 “雷格利乌斯。雷格利乌斯。雷格利乌斯，”斯科提不敢喊出声来。在这一分钟之间，枝杈传来一阵颤动，那位瓦内奇大人的幕僚现身而出。先是背包，再是头，然后是余下的身形全貌。斯科提开始压低嗓子传话，但雷格利乌斯拼命摇头，向下一望。一只卡吉特正盘踞于大树根底，对着营火的余痕虎视眈眈。 雷格利乌斯手脚笨拙地试图在枝头保持平衡，然而尽管枝干强壮坚实，想要仅在空出一只手的情况下保持平衡也万分困难。斯科提将手掌合捧着，对着那背包使了个眼色。对于雷格利乌斯而言，松手放开背包似乎是一个无比痛苦的决定，但他最终妥协了，将包朝着斯科提投掷了过去。 在背包上，有一处微不可见的破口，而当斯科提接住了背包的时候，一枚金币从包里掉了出来。它在下坠过程中与石壁磕碰弹起，叩击出清脆的声响，这声音高亢又轻柔，仿佛是斯科提有生以来听到的最为刺耳的警报声。 一切发生得太快。 树下的卡塞-拉特目光上巡，猛然怒号一声。随着那只下方的猫俯身一跃，攀上了低矮处的树枝，余下的卡吉特纷纷啸聚而应。雷格利乌斯看见它从自己下方以难以置信的灵敏向上攀跃，顿时陷入了恐惧。甚至在他起跳之前，斯科提也能判断出他将会坠落下去。伴随着一声尖叫，文员雷格利乌斯猛然下坠，颈部受冲力一挫，立刻折断。 一道白色光焰骤然从遍布神庙的裂隙间喷涌而出，而波斯莫祈祷者们的呻吟开始转变为某种仿佛来自异界的可怖声响。那只向上攀爬的卡塞-拉特停止了爬行，凝望着神庙。 “凯尔苟啊，”它倒吸一口凉气。“是狂猎。” 似乎在现实中被劈开一条缝隙。大群生着可怖面貌的异兽浩荡奔涌而出，蟾蜍遍体触须，昆虫披甲带刺，巨蛇长躯似胶，更有那聚气为形的神头鬼脸之辈，暴怒难遏，自巨大圣树间倾巢而出。它们撕碎了神庙前的卡吉特。余下的猫纷纷向丛林窜逃，然而在它们逃亡的同时，也开始拉拽起各自携带着的绳索。数秒之后，整座温蒂斯村就被一群癫狂错乱的狂猎鬼怪搅得喧嚣如沸。 一片嘈杂吼啸与群声嚣叫中，斯科提听着藏身的希罗帝尔人在被吞食时惨嚎出声。诺德人同样被搜捉出来果腹，那些布莱顿人也遭遇了同样的境遇。尽管巫师施法将自己隐形，然而兽群并不依赖视觉掠食。那株卡塞-拉特容身的树木被一股极大的巨力所摧折，开始剧烈摇曳晃动起来。斯科提看见了卡吉特被恐惧侵袭的双眼，随后递出了一根苔藓编连的长条。 当那只猫跳向藤蔓时，它的面孔上显露出令人哀悯的感激之意。斯科提往回拉扯着藤条时，它脸上还保持着那副感激神态，接着，斯科提就看见它跌落了下去。在它触地之前，狂猎就将他一身血肉吞噬殆尽，只留下骨架一具。 斯科提自己尝试跳向下一处耸峙而出的露岩，而这一次则要成功得多。在那里，他竭力拖着身体爬到了悬崖巅峰，于此处便可俯瞰那片由温蒂斯村化作的暴乱。一众狂猎数量愈增，逐渐自出峡谷的道路涌出，追猎逃亡的卡吉特。那时才是疯嚣肆虐之始。 借着月色，从斯科提居高临下的位置看去，他得以观察到卡吉特在何处缠缚着它们的绳索。随着一声轰鸣震响如雷，宛如雪崩一般的坍塌自山体爆发，挟着滚滚山石倾泻而下，阻绝了道路。待烟尘肃清，他才望见整座村庄已被坍塌的巨石封锁了出路，失去捕杀猎物的出路，狂猎只能开始自相残杀。 斯科提偏过头来，不忍再目睹这同类相食的狂欢。夜色中的丛林矗立其后，一片密林交织成网。他一把将雷格利乌斯的背包挂在肩上，走了进去。

[pagebreak]

a dance in fire chapter 4 by waughin jarth

[pagebreak]

ighteen bosmeri and one cyrodilic former senior clerk for an imperial building commission trudged through the jungle westward from the xylo river to the ancient village of vindisi. for decumus scotti, the jungle was hostile, unfamiliar ground. the enormous vermiculated trees filled the bright morning with darkness, and resembled nothing so much as grasping claws, bent on impeding their progress. even the fronds of the low plants quivered with malevolent energy. what was worse, he was not alone in his anxiety. his fellow travelers, the natives who had survived the khajiit attacks on the villages of grenos and athay, wore faces of undisguised fear. there was something sentient in the jungle, and not merely the mad but benevolent indigenous spirits. in his peripheral vision, scotti could see the shadows of the khajiiti following the refugees, leaping from tree to tree. when he turned to face them, the lithe forms vanished into the gloom as if they had never been there. but he knew he had seen them. and the bosmeri saw them too, and quickened their pace. after eighteen hours, bitten raw by insects, scratched by a thousand thorns, they emerged into a valley clearing. it was night, but a row of blazing torches greeted them, illuminating the leather-wrought tents and jumbled stones of the hamlet of vindisi. at the end of the valley, the torches marked a sacred site, a gnarled bower of trees pressed closed together to form a temple. wordlessly, the bosmeri walked the torch arcade toward the trees. scotti followed them. when they reached the solid mass of living wood with only one gaping portal, scotti could see a dim blue light glowing within. a low sonorous moan from a hundred voices echoed within. the bosmeri maiden he had been following held out her hand, stopping him. "you do not understand, but no outsider, not even a friend may enter," she said. "this is a holy place." scotti nodded, and watched the refugees march into the temple, heads bowed. their voices joined with the ones within. when the last wood elf had gone inside, scotti turned his attention back to the village. there must be food to be had somewhere. a tendril of smoke and a faint whiff of roasting venison beyond the torchlight led him. they were five cyrodiils, two bretons, and a nord, the group gathered around a campfire of glowing white stones, pulling steaming strips of meat from the cadaver of a great stag. at scotti's approach, they rose up, all but the nord who was distracted by his hunk of animal flesh. "good evening, sorry to interrupt, but i was wondering if i might have a little something to eat. i'm afraid i'm rather hungry, after walking all day with some refugees from grenos and athay." they bade him to sit down and eat, and introduced themselves. "so the war's back on, it seems," said scotti amiably. "best thing for these effete do-nothings," replied the nord in between bites. "i've never seen such a lazy culture. now they've got the khajiiti striking them on land, and the high elves at sea. if there's any province that deserves a little distress, it's damnable valenwood." "i don't see how they're so offensive to you," laughed one of the bretons. "they're congenital thieves, even worse than the khajiiti because they are so blessed meek in their aggression," the nord spat out a gob of fat which sizzled on the hot stones of the fire. "they spread their forests into territory that doesn't belong to them, slowly infiltrating their neighbors, and they're puzzled when elsweyr shoves back at them. they're all villains of the worst order." "what are you doing here?" asked scotti. "i'm a diplomat from the court of jehenna," muttered the nord, returning to his food. "what about you, what are you doing here?" asked one of the cyrodiils. "i work for lord atrius's building commission in the imperial city," said scotti. "one of my former colleagues suggested that i come down to valenwood. he said the war was over, and i could contract a great deal of business for my firm rebuilding what was lost. one disaster after another, and i've lost all my money, i'm in the middle of a rekindling of war, and i cannot find my former colleague." "your former colleague," murmured another of the cyrodiils, who had introduced himself as reglius. "he wasn't by any chance named liodes jurus, was he?" "you know him?" "he lured me down to valenwood in nearly the exact same circumstances," smiled reglius, grimly. "i worked for your employer's competitor, lord vanech's men, where liodes jurus also formerly worked. he wrote to me, asking that i represent an imperial building commission and contract some post-war construction. i had just been released from my employment, and i thought that if i brought some new business, i could have my job back. jurus and i met in athay, and he said he was going to arrange a very lucrative meeting with the silvenar." scotti was stunned: "where is he now?" "i'm no theologian, so i couldn't say," reglius shrugged. "he's dead. when the khajiiti attacked athay, they began by torching the harbor where jurus was readying his boat. or, i should say, my boat since it was purchased with the gold i brought. by the time we were even aware of what was happening enough to flee, everything by the water was ash. the khajiiti may be animals, but they know how to arrange an attack." "i think they followed us through the jungle to vindisi," said scotti nervously. "there was definitely a group of something jumping along the treetops." "probably one of the monkey folk," snorted the nord. "nothing to be concerned about." "when we first came to vindisi and the bosmeri all entered that tree, they were furious, whispering something about unleashing an ancient terror on their enemies," the breton shivered, remembering. "they've been there ever since, for over a day and a half now. if you want something to be afraid of, that's the direction to look." the other breton, who was a representative of the daggerfall mages guild, was staring off into the darkness while his fellow provincial spoke. "maybe. but there's something in the jungle too, right on the edge of the village, looking in." "more refugees maybe?" asked scotti, trying to keep the alarm out his voice. "not unless they're traveling through the trees now," whispered the wizard. the nord and one of the cyrodiils grabbed a long tarp of wet leather and pulled it across the fire, instantly extinguishing it without so much as a sizzle. now scotti could see the intruders, their elliptical yellow eyes and long cruel blades catching the torchlight. he froze with fear, praying that he too was not so visible to them. he felt something bump against his back, and gasped. reglius's voice hissed from up above: "be quiet for mara's sake and climb up here." scotti grabbed hold of the knotted double-vine that hung down from a tall tree at the edge of the dead campfire. he scrambled up it as quickly as he could, holding his breath lest any grunt of exertion escape him. at the top of the vine, high above the village, was an abandoned nest from some great bird in a trident-shaped branch. as soon as scotti had pulled himself into the soft, fragrant straw, reglius pulled up the vine. no one else was there, and when scotti looked down, he could see no one below. no one, that is except the khajiiti, slowly moving toward the glow of the temple tree. "thank you," whispered scotti, deeply touched that a competitor had helped him. he turned away from the village, and saw that the tree's upper branches brushed against the mossy rock walls that surrounded the valley below. "how are you at climbing?" "you're mad," said reglius under his breath. "we should stay here until they leave." "if they burn vindisi like they did athay and grenos, we'll be dead sure as if we were on the ground," scotti began the slow careful climb up the tree, testing each branch. "can you see what they're doing?" "i can't really tell," reglius stared down into the gloom. "they're at the front of the temple. i think they also have ... it looks like long ropes, trailing off behind them, off into the pass." scotti crawled onto the strongest branch that pointed toward the wet, rocky face of the cliff. it was not a far jump at all. so close, in fact, that he could smell the moisture and feel the coolness of the stone. but it was a jump nevertheless, and in his history as a clerk, he had never before leapt from a tree a hundred feet off the ground to a sheer rock. he pictured in his mind's eye the shadows that had pursued him through the jungle from the heights above. how their legs coiled to spring, how their arms snapped forward in an elegant fluid motion to grasp. he leapt. his hands grappled for rock, but long thick cords of moss were more accessible. he held hard, but when he tried to plant his feet forward, they slipped up skyward. for a few seconds, he found himself upside down before he managed to pull himself into a more conventional position. there was a narrow outcropping jutting out of the cliff where he could stand and finally exhale. "reglius. reglius. reglius," scotti did not dare to call out. in a minute, there was a shaking of branches, and lord vanech's man emerged. first his satchel, then his head, then the rest of him. scotti started to whisper something, but reglius shook his head violently and pointed downward. one of the khajiiti was at the base of the tree, peering at the remains of the campfire. reglius awkwardly tried to balance himself on the branch, but as strong as it was it was exceedingly difficult with only one free hand. scotti cupped his palms and then pointed at the satchel. it seemed to pain reglius to let it out of his grasp, but he relented and tossed it to scotti. there was a small, almost invisible hole in the bag, and when scotti caught it, a single gold coin dropped out. it rang as it bounced against the rock wall on the descent, a high soft sound that seemed like the loudest alarm scotti had ever heard. then many things happened very quickly. the cathay-raht at the base of the tree looked up and gave a loud wail. the other khajiiti followed in chorus, as the cat below crouched down and then sprung up into the lower branches. reglius saw it below him, climbing up with impossible dexterity, and panicked. even before he jumped, scotti could tell that he was going to fall. with a cry, reglius the clerk plunged to the ground, breaking his neck on impact. a flash of white fire erupted from every crevice of the temple, and the moan of the bosmeri prayer changed into something terrible and otherworldly. the climbing cathay-raht stopped and stared. "keirgo," it gasped. "the wild hunt." it was as if a crack in reality had opened wide. a flood of horrific beasts, tentacled toads, insects of armor and spine, gelatinous serpents, vaporous beings with the face of gods, all poured forth from the great hollow tree, blind with fury. they tore the khajiiti in front of the temple to pieces. all the other cats fled for the jungle, but as they did so, they began pulling on the ropes they carried. in a few seconds time, the entire village of vindisi was boiling with the lunatic apparitions of the wild hunt. over the babbling, barking, howling horde, scotti heard the cyrodiils in hiding cry out as they were devoured. the nord too was found and eaten, and both bretons. the wizard had turned himself invisible, but the swarm did not rely on their sight. the tree the cathay-raht was in began to sway and rock from the impossible violence beneath it. scotti looked at the khajiiti's fear-struck eyes, and held out one of the cords of moss. the cat's face showed its pitiful gratitude as it leapt for the vine. it didn't have time to entirely replace that expression when scotti pulled back the cord, and watched it fall. the hunt consumed it to the bone before it struck the ground. scotti's own jump up to the next outcropping of rock was immeasurably more successful. from there, he pulled himself to the top of the cliff and was able to look down into the chaos that had been the village of vindisi. the hunt's mass had grown and began to spill out through the pass out of the valley, pursuing the fleeing khajiiti. it was then that the madness truly began. in the moons' light, from scotti's vantage, he could see where the khajiiti had attached their ropes. with a thunderous boom, an avalanche of boulders poured over the pass. when the dust cleared, he saw that the valley had been sealed. the wild hunt had nowhere to turn but on itself. scotti turned his head, unable to bear to look at the cannibalistic orgy. the night jungle stood before him, a web of wood. he slung reglius's satchel over his shoulder, and entered.
chineseNormalized:
[pagebreak]

火中舞 卷四 瓦辛·贾斯 著

[pagebreak]

十八位波斯莫和一位曾于帝都建筑委员会就职的希罗帝尔文员，自木河向古老村落温蒂斯经，沿途穿林过木、步履沉重地跋涉了许久。对于戴奎莫斯·斯科提来说，这片丛林是一片饱含着敌意的陌生土地。有大量经虫蛀的繁茂树木掩住了晨曦，活像是攫取猎食的螯爪，一心阻碍着他们前进。甚至是低矮植物的叶片，也不怀好意地颤动不止。更糟的是，不单是斯科提一人焦虑难安。他的同行者们，这些在卡吉特对格仁诺斯和艾赛的进攻中幸存下来的本地居民，脸上纷纷不加掩饰地流露出恐惧之色。 不独是疯疯癫癫却心地善良的本地生灵，在树丛中尚有其它活物。在他的视野外围，斯科提能看见那些阴影时现，那属于尾随着难民而来的卡吉特，他们在树木上腾跃辗转。当他把脸转向他们时，那些矫健灵活的身影便消失在了黑暗中，仿佛从未存在过一般。可是他知道自己确实亲眼目击了他们。那些波斯莫也看见了，所以加快了步伐。 在十八小时后，被昆虫叮咬着伤口、被密密麻麻的千万道荆棘割伤的他们进入了从一片山谷空地。此时已经入夜，但有一排灼灼燃烧的火炬向着他们迎了过来，照亮了革质帐篷和温蒂斯村遍地随意堆砌的石块。在山谷尽头，火炬之光照耀着一处圣坛，森森树影中，无数枝条彼此相依，拱卫成一座神庙。无需多言，波斯莫们穿过设有火炬的廊道，向着树木走去。斯科提紧随其后。当他们到达这根茎坚固、撑天拄地的活体树木时，仅有一道大门洞开，斯科提能看见昏昧幽暗的蓝光在其中闪烁。百种声调齐鸣而起，汇合成一阵低沉浑厚的哀嚎，在洞中震起无数回音。那位始终带着他前行的波斯莫少女伸出手阻住了他。 “你不会明白的，但没有任何外人得以进入，甚至是朋友也不行。”她说。“这是一座圣地。” 斯科提略一颔首，看着难民们从神庙口低垂着头，鱼贯而入。他们的声音混入了洞中的回响。当最后一个木精灵进入到洞中，斯科提便将注意力转回了村庄。某些地方肯定还储存着食物。缭绕若丝的烟尘和些微烤鹿肉的气息越过火炬的光芒，引着他向气味发出的方向走去。 五名希罗帝尔人、两名布莱顿人和一名诺德人围着一簇营火聚集着。那火将白色的石头烧得发红。他们从一头巨大雄鹿尸体上撕扯下一条条散发着热气的肉片。在斯科提走近他们时，除了一位专心于自己那一大块兽肉的诺德人外，其他人都纷纷站了起来。 “晚上好，很抱歉冒昧打扰，但我想问问能否分我一点吃食。我和格仁诺斯与艾赛的难民们徒步走了整整一天，现在很饿了。” 他们招呼他坐下来用餐，并且各自做了自我介绍。 “看来战争是卷土重来了。”斯科提语气友善地发问道。 “现在这些废物蛋啥都别去惹，就谢天谢地了。”诺德人在咀嚼之余回应道，“我就没瞧见过这么懒散的文化。现在陆上有卡吉特，海上又有高精灵袭击他们。如果有哪个省份活该吃点小苦头，那铁定就是这天杀的威伦森林。” “我可没看见他们有冒犯过你。”布莱顿人中的一位大笑道。 “他们天生就是小贼，简直比卡吉特还坏，连侵略的时候也总是一副乖头乖脑的样子，”诺德人吐出一大块肥肉，那脂肪掉在了被火烤至高温的石头上，“滋滋”一声，发出一阵火焰燃烧油脂的轻响。“他们把自家的森林扩张到不属于他们的领土上，一点点渗透进他们的邻居，艾斯维尔反攻回来他们还摸不着头脑。他们都是些活在最差劲的社会里的混蛋。” “你在这里做什么呢？”斯科提发问。 “我是约赫纳宫廷派来的外交使臣。”诺德人嘀咕着说了一句，又回去吃他的食物。 “那你呢，你是来这干什么的？”其中一个希罗帝尔人问。 “我为帝都的阿特里乌斯大人的建筑委会工作。”斯科提说。“我的一位前同事建议我南下威伦森林。他说战争已经结束，于是我能够借助重修那些在战争中毁于一旦的建筑，为我的公司签下一份满是商机的大合同。灾厄接踵而至，我失去了所有钱财，还跑到了战火重燃的核心地区，而且，我还是没能找到我的前任同事。” “你的前任同事，”另一位自称雷格利乌斯的希罗帝尔人喃喃低语道，“他不会正好就叫利奥德斯·朱鲁斯吧，是他吗？” “你认识他？ “在我混得和你一模一样的时候，他把我引到南部的威伦森林。”雷格利乌斯冷冷地笑道，“我给你老板的竞争对头瓦内奇大人的手下工作，那也是利奥德斯·朱鲁斯曾经工作过的地方。他写信给我，让我代表帝国建筑委会，来签订某种战后重修的合约。我刚刚被解雇，还在想着如果我能带来些新的生意，我就能夺回我的工作。朱鲁斯同我在艾赛会面，并说他要安排一场和希尔维纳的商谈，这其中有大生意可做。” 斯科提诧异不已：“那他现在在哪？” “我不是神学家，所以我也不知道，”雷格利乌斯耸了耸肩。“他死了。在卡吉特们攻打艾赛的时候，他们开始焚烧港口，朱鲁斯那时候正准备着登上他的船离开。或者我应该说，我的船，因为那是花了我的金币买到的。甚至在我们充分意识到发生的事情从而逃离之前，水边的一切便焚作齑粉。卡吉特们可能是群畜生，但是他们很懂得怎么安排进攻。” “我认为他们已经尾随我们穿行过丛林，来到了温蒂斯，”斯科提紧张地陈述着，“在树梢之间肯定有什么生物在成群结队地跳跃。” “可能就是些猴人。”诺德人打了个喷嚏。“没啥好担心的。” “在我们刚到温蒂斯的时候，波斯莫全部进入了那棵树，他们躁动不安，低语着要向敌人释放一种古老的恐怖。”布莱顿人打着战回忆道。“他们就一直站在那，站了足足有一天半了。如果你想找点让你害怕的东西，那就往他们那个方向看一眼。” 另一位布莱顿人，即那位匕落法师公会的代表，在他的同乡发言时，目光茫然地盯着一片黑暗。“可能是吧。但是丛林里也有东西，就在村庄边缘，在向我们窥视。” “可能是其他的难民吧？”斯科提问道，视着让自己保持平静的口吻。 “除非他们现在正穿过树行进，”巫师悄声说着。诺德人和一名希罗帝尔人抓过湿皮革制成的长条油布，将它拉过来盖在了火上，火焰霎时熄灭，连一丝轻微的烧灼声也没有传出。现在斯科提可以看见那些不速之客，它们呈椭圆的黄色眼瞳，以及反射着火光、修长而嗜血的剑刃。他全身冻结在恐惧中，动弹不得，祈祷着自己在它们看来不要太过显眼。 他感到有什么东西碰了碰他的后背，不由倒吸一口凉气。 雷格利乌斯的声音嘶嘶地从上方传来：“看在玛拉的份上，给我安静点，从这爬上来。” 在业已熄灭的营火边缘，从一株高大树木上垂下一根由两道藤蔓绞缠而成的绳子，斯科提一把抓住绳子，全速地向上攀爬，小心地将呼吸放轻，以防有任何喘息声从他口鼻中逃逸出去。在藤蔓的最上端，有一座荒废的巨鸟巢穴坐落在如三叉戟一般的枝杈上，高悬于村庄上空。斯科提刚刚一头栽倒在散发着馥郁气息的柔软麦秆上，就看见雷格利乌斯将藤蔓拉上来收起。下方现已不余一人，而后，在斯科提俯瞰下视时，他也没有看到有任何人影。除了那些朝着那座树木神庙的光辉缓步移动的卡吉特。 “谢谢你，”斯科提耳语道，对这位曾经的竞争者予以他的帮助，内心触动不已。他从村庄方向转过头，看见树木上部的枝柯轻轻触着环抱谷底、生满苔藓的石壁。“你攀爬能力如何？” “你疯了吧，”雷格利乌斯按低了呼吸声说道。“我们就该在这待到他们离开。” “如果他们焚毁了温蒂斯，就像他们对艾赛和格仁诺斯所做的那样，我们下地就死定了。”斯科提开始小心缓慢地尝试爬上树，尝试着每一根枝条是否能供攀登。“你能看见他们在干什么吗？” “说实话，我看不太出来，”雷格利乌斯望向那一片幽暗中。“他们聚集在神庙前方。我想他们也带有……那看起来就像长绳，拖行在他们身后，一直延伸到路上。” 斯科提攀上了一根最为坚固粗硕的枝条，那枝条延伸向潮湿嶙峋的悬崖表面。想跳上崖岸，他所需要跃过的距离并不算远。实际上，这距离近得能让他嗅到丝丝水汽，切身感受到崖石间沁出的阴冷寒意。然而这依旧需要他跳出一步，而在他文员生涯中，他从未做过自去地百尺的树梢间腾跃上陡峭岩石这种事。在他的脑海中，浮现出了那些腾挪于树梢高处、穿林过叶追逐着他的身影。他想起了他们的腿部如何弯曲再发力跳起，他们的手臂如何前伸，优雅灵敏地抓稳树枝。然后，他一步跃起。 他的手努力够向岩石，然而更易于攫获的却是以长条带状生长出的茁茂苔藓。他紧紧抓握住苔藓须茎，然而当他尝试着将双足前蹬时，却不慎失足，两脚朝天。他发现自己上下颠倒，倒悬了几秒，直到他扯着苔藓，将自己拽到一个更习惯的姿势。一道狭窄的露头岩石自峭壁上支起，他得以立足其上，并在落脚后松了口气。 “雷格利乌斯。雷格利乌斯。雷格利乌斯，”斯科提不敢喊出声来。在这一分钟之间，枝杈传来一阵颤动，那位瓦内奇大人的幕僚现身而出。先是背包，再是头，然后是余下的身形全貌。斯科提开始压低嗓子传话，但雷格利乌斯拼命摇头，向下一望。一只卡吉特正盘踞于大树根底，对着营火的余痕虎视眈眈。 雷格利乌斯手脚笨拙地试图在枝头保持平衡，然而尽管枝干强壮坚实，想要仅在空出一只手的情况下保持平衡也万分困难。斯科提将手掌合捧着，对着那背包使了个眼色。对于雷格利乌斯而言，松手放开背包似乎是一个无比痛苦的决定，但他最终妥协了，将包朝着斯科提投掷了过去。 在背包上，有一处微不可见的破口，而当斯科提接住了背包的时候，一枚金币从包里掉了出来。它在下坠过程中与石壁磕碰弹起，叩击出清脆的声响，这声音高亢又轻柔，仿佛是斯科提有生以来听到的最为刺耳的警报声。 一切发生得太快。 树下的卡塞-拉特目光上巡，猛然怒号一声。随着那只下方的猫俯身一跃，攀上了低矮处的树枝，余下的卡吉特纷纷啸聚而应。雷格利乌斯看见它从自己下方以难以置信的灵敏向上攀跃，顿时陷入了恐惧。甚至在他起跳之前，斯科提也能判断出他将会坠落下去。伴随着一声尖叫，文员雷格利乌斯猛然下坠，颈部受冲力一挫，立刻折断。 一道白色光焰骤然从遍布神庙的裂隙间喷涌而出，而波斯莫祈祷者们的呻吟开始转变为某种仿佛来自异界的可怖声响。那只向上攀爬的卡塞-拉特停止了爬行，凝望着神庙。 “凯尔苟啊，”它倒吸一口凉气。“是狂猎。” 似乎在现实中被劈开一条缝隙。大群生着可怖面貌的异兽浩荡奔涌而出，蟾蜍遍体触须，昆虫披甲带刺，巨蛇长躯似胶，更有那聚气为形的神头鬼脸之辈，暴怒难遏，自巨大圣树间倾巢而出。它们撕碎了神庙前的卡吉特。余下的猫纷纷向丛林窜逃，然而在它们逃亡的同时，也开始拉拽起各自携带着的绳索。数秒之后，整座温蒂斯村就被一群癫狂错乱的狂猎鬼怪搅得喧嚣如沸。 一片嘈杂吼啸与群声嚣叫中，斯科提听着藏身的希罗帝尔人在被吞食时惨嚎出声。诺德人同样被搜捉出来果腹，那些布莱顿人也遭遇了同样的境遇。尽管巫师施法将自己隐形，然而兽群并不依赖视觉掠食。那株卡塞-拉特容身的树木被一股极大的巨力所摧折，开始剧烈摇曳晃动起来。斯科提看见了卡吉特被恐惧侵袭的双眼，随后递出了一根苔藓编连的长条。 当那只猫跳向藤蔓时，它的面孔上显露出令人哀悯的感激之意。斯科提往回拉扯着藤条时，它脸上还保持着那副感激神态，接着，斯科提就看见它跌落了下去。在它触地之前，狂猎就将他一身血肉吞噬殆尽，只留下骨架一具。 斯科提自己尝试跳向下一处耸峙而出的露岩，而这一次则要成功得多。在那里，他竭力拖着身体爬到了悬崖巅峰，于此处便可俯瞰那片由温蒂斯村化作的暴乱。一众狂猎数量愈增，逐渐自出峡谷的道路涌出，追猎逃亡的卡吉特。那时才是疯嚣肆虐之始。 借着月色，从斯科提居高临下的位置看去，他得以观察到卡吉特在何处缠缚着它们的绳索。随着一声轰鸣震响如雷，宛如雪崩一般的坍塌自山体爆发，挟着滚滚山石倾泻而下，阻绝了道路。待烟尘肃清，他才望见整座村庄已被坍塌的巨石封锁了出路，失去捕杀猎物的出路，狂猎只能开始自相残杀。 斯科提偏过头来，不忍再目睹这同类相食的狂欢。夜色中的丛林矗立其后，一片密林交织成网。他一把将雷格利乌斯的背包挂在肩上，走了进去。
agentText:
Addon: Skyrim EDID: Book0DanceInFireV4 Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 0095BB English: [pagebreak]

A Dance in Fire Chapter 4 by Waughin Jarth

[pagebreak]

ighteen Bosmeri and one Cyrodilic former senior clerk for an Imperial building commission trudged through the jungle westward from the Xylo River to the ancient village of Vindisi. For Decumus Scotti, the jungle was hostile, unfamiliar ground. The enormous vermiculated trees filled the bright morning with darkness, and resembled nothing so much as grasping claws, bent on impeding their progress. Even the fronds of the low plants quivered with malevolent energy. What was worse, he was not alone in his anxiety. His fellow travelers, the natives who had survived the Khajiit attacks on the villages of Grenos and Athay, wore faces of undisguised fear. There was something sentient in the jungle, and not merely the mad but benevolent indigenous spirits. In his peripheral vision, Scotti could see the shadows of the Khajiiti following the refugees, leaping from tree to tree. When he turned to face them, the lithe forms vanished into the gloom as if they had never been there. But he knew he had seen them. And the Bosmeri saw them too, and quickened their pace. After eighteen hours, bitten raw by insects, scratched by a thousand thorns, they emerged into a valley clearing. It was night, but a row of blazing torches greeted them, illuminating the leather-wrought tents and jumbled stones of the hamlet of Vindisi. At the end of the valley, the torches marked a sacred site, a gnarled bower of trees pressed closed together to form a temple. Wordlessly, the Bosmeri walked the torch arcade toward the trees. Scotti followed them. When they reached the solid mass of living wood with only one gaping portal, Scotti could see a dim blue light glowing within. A low sonorous moan from a hundred voices echoed within. The Bosmeri maiden he had been following held out her hand, stopping him. "You do not understand, but no outsider, not even a friend may enter," she said. "This is a holy place." Scotti nodded, and watched the refugees march into the temple, heads bowed. Their voices joined with the ones within. When the last wood elf had gone inside, Scotti turned his attention back to the village. There must be food to be had somewhere. A tendril of smoke and a faint whiff of roasting venison beyond the torchlight led him. They were five Cyrodiils, two Bretons, and a Nord, the group gathered around a campfire of glowing white stones, pulling steaming strips of meat from the cadaver of a great stag. At Scotti's approach, they rose up, all but the Nord who was distracted by his hunk of animal flesh. "Good evening, sorry to interrupt, but I was wondering if I might have a little something to eat. I'm afraid I'm rather hungry, after walking all day with some refugees from Grenos and Athay." They bade him to sit down and eat, and introduced themselves. "So the war's back on, it seems," said Scotti amiably. "Best thing for these effete do-nothings," replied the Nord in between bites. "I've never seen such a lazy culture. Now they've got the Khajiiti striking them on land, and the high elves at sea. If there's any province that deserves a little distress, it's damnable Valenwood." "I don't see how they're so offensive to you," laughed one of the Bretons. "They're congenital thieves, even worse than the Khajiiti because they are so blessed meek in their aggression," the Nord spat out a gob of fat which sizzled on the hot stones of the fire. "They spread their forests into territory that doesn't belong to them, slowly infiltrating their neighbors, and they're puzzled when Elsweyr shoves back at them. They're all villains of the worst order." "What are you doing here?" asked Scotti. "I'm a diplomat from the court of Jehenna," muttered the Nord, returning to his food. "What about you, what are you doing here?" asked one of the Cyrodiils. "I work for Lord Atrius's building commission in the Imperial City," said Scotti. "One of my former colleagues suggested that I come down to Valenwood. He said the war was over, and I could contract a great deal of business for my firm rebuilding what was lost. One disaster after another, and I've lost all my money, I'm in the middle of a rekindling of war, and I cannot find my former colleague." "Your former colleague," murmured another of the Cyrodiils, who had introduced himself as Reglius. "He wasn't by any chance named Liodes Jurus, was he?" "You know him?" "He lured me down to Valenwood in nearly the exact same circumstances," smiled Reglius, grimly. "I worked for your employer's competitor, Lord Vanech's men, where Liodes Jurus also formerly worked. He wrote to me, asking that I represent an Imperial building commission and contract some post-war construction. I had just been released from my employment, and I thought that if I brought some new business, I could have my job back. Jurus and I met in Athay, and he said he was going to arrange a very lucrative meeting with the Silvenar." Scotti was stunned: "Where is he now?" "I'm no theologian, so I couldn't say," Reglius shrugged. "He's dead. When the Khajiiti attacked Athay, they began by torching the harbor where Jurus was readying his boat. Or, I should say, my boat since it was purchased with the gold I brought. By the time we were even aware of what was happening enough to flee, everything by the water was ash. The Khajiiti may be animals, but they know how to arrange an attack." "I think they followed us through the jungle to Vindisi," said Scotti nervously. "There was definitely a group of something jumping along the treetops." "Probably one of the monkey folk," snorted the Nord. "Nothing to be concerned about." "When we first came to Vindisi and the Bosmeri all entered that tree, they were furious, whispering something about unleashing an ancient terror on their enemies," the Breton shivered, remembering. "They've been there ever since, for over a day and a half now. If you want something to be afraid of, that's the direction to look." The other Breton, who was a representative of the Daggerfall Mages Guild, was staring off into the darkness while his fellow provincial spoke. "Maybe. But there's something in the jungle too, right on the edge of the village, looking in." "More refugees maybe?" asked Scotti, trying to keep the alarm out his voice. "Not unless they're traveling through the trees now," whispered the wizard. The Nord and one of the Cyrodiils grabbed a long tarp of wet leather and pulled it across the fire, instantly extinguishing it without so much as a sizzle. Now Scotti could see the intruders, their elliptical yellow eyes and long cruel blades catching the torchlight. He froze with fear, praying that he too was not so visible to them. He felt something bump against his back, and gasped. Reglius's voice hissed from up above: "Be quiet for Mara's sake and climb up here." Scotti grabbed hold of the knotted double-vine that hung down from a tall tree at the edge of the dead campfire. He scrambled up it as quickly as he could, holding his breath lest any grunt of exertion escape him. At the top of the vine, high above the village, was an abandoned nest from some great bird in a trident-shaped branch. As soon as Scotti had pulled himself into the soft, fragrant straw, Reglius pulled up the vine. No one else was there, and when Scotti looked down, he could see no one below. No one, that is except the Khajiiti, slowly moving toward the glow of the temple tree. "Thank you," whispered Scotti, deeply touched that a competitor had helped him. He turned away from the village, and saw that the tree's upper branches brushed against the mossy rock walls that surrounded the valley below. "How are you at climbing?" "You're mad," said Reglius under his breath. "We should stay here until they leave." "If they burn Vindisi like they did Athay and Grenos, we'll be dead sure as if we were on the ground," Scotti began the slow careful climb up the tree, testing each branch. "Can you see what they're doing?" "I can't really tell," Reglius stared down into the gloom. "They're at the front of the temple. I think they also have ... it looks like long ropes, trailing off behind them, off into the pass." Scotti crawled onto the strongest branch that pointed toward the wet, rocky face of the cliff. It was not a far jump at all. So close, in fact, that he could smell the moisture and feel the coolness of the stone. But it was a jump nevertheless, and in his history as a clerk, he had never before leapt from a tree a hundred feet off the ground to a sheer rock. He pictured in his mind's eye the shadows that had pursued him through the jungle from the heights above. How their legs coiled to spring, how their arms snapped forward in an elegant fluid motion to grasp. He leapt. His hands grappled for rock, but long thick cords of moss were more accessible. He held hard, but when he tried to plant his feet forward, they slipped up skyward. For a few seconds, he found himself upside down before he managed to pull himself into a more conventional position. There was a narrow outcropping jutting out of the cliff where he could stand and finally exhale. "Reglius. Reglius. Reglius," Scotti did not dare to call out. In a minute, there was a shaking of branches, and Lord Vanech's man emerged. First his satchel, then his head, then the rest of him. Scotti started to whisper something, but Reglius shook his head violently and pointed downward. One of the Khajiiti was at the base of the tree, peering at the remains of the campfire. Reglius awkwardly tried to balance himself on the branch, but as strong as it was it was exceedingly difficult with only one free hand. Scotti cupped his palms and then pointed at the satchel. It seemed to pain Reglius to let it out of his grasp, but he relented and tossed it to Scotti. There was a small, almost invisible hole in the bag, and when Scotti caught it, a single gold coin dropped out. It rang as it bounced against the rock wall on the descent, a high soft sound that seemed like the loudest alarm Scotti had ever heard. Then many things happened very quickly. The Cathay-Raht at the base of the tree looked up and gave a loud wail. The other Khajiiti followed in chorus, as the cat below crouched down and then sprung up into the lower branches. Reglius saw it below him, climbing up with impossible dexterity, and panicked. Even before he jumped, Scotti could tell that he was going to fall. With a cry, Reglius the Clerk plunged to the ground, breaking his neck on impact. A flash of white fire erupted from every crevice of the temple, and the moan of the Bosmeri prayer changed into something terrible and otherworldly. The climbing Cathay-Raht stopped and stared. "Keirgo," it gasped. "The Wild Hunt." It was as if a crack in reality had opened wide. A flood of horrific beasts, tentacled toads, insects of armor and spine, gelatinous serpents, vaporous beings with the face of gods, all poured forth from the great hollow tree, blind with fury. They tore the Khajiiti in front of the temple to pieces. All the other cats fled for the jungle, but as they did so, they began pulling on the ropes they carried. In a few seconds time, the entire village of Vindisi was boiling with the lunatic apparitions of the Wild Hunt. Over the babbling, barking, howling horde, Scotti heard the Cyrodiils in hiding cry out as they were devoured. The Nord too was found and eaten, and both Bretons. The wizard had turned himself invisible, but the swarm did not rely on their sight. The tree the Cathay-Raht was in began to sway and rock from the impossible violence beneath it. Scotti looked at the Khajiiti's fear-struck eyes, and held out one of the cords of moss. The cat's face showed its pitiful gratitude as it leapt for the vine. It didn't have time to entirely replace that expression when Scotti pulled back the cord, and watched it fall. The Hunt consumed it to the bone before it struck the ground. Scotti's own jump up to the next outcropping of rock was immeasurably more successful. From there, he pulled himself to the top of the cliff and was able to look down into the chaos that had been the village of Vindisi. The Hunt's mass had grown and began to spill out through the pass out of the valley, pursuing the fleeing Khajiiti. It was then that the madness truly began. In the moons' light, from Scotti's vantage, he could see where the Khajiiti had attached their ropes. With a thunderous boom, an avalanche of boulders poured over the pass. When the dust cleared, he saw that the valley had been sealed. The Wild Hunt had nowhere to turn but on itself. Scotti turned his head, unable to bear to look at the cannibalistic orgy. The night jungle stood before him, a web of wood. He slung Reglius's satchel over his shoulder, and entered.

Chinese: [pagebreak]

火中舞 卷四 瓦辛·贾斯 著

[pagebreak]

十八位波斯莫和一位曾于帝都建筑委员会就职的希罗帝尔文员，自木河向古老村落温蒂斯经，沿途穿林过木、步履沉重地跋涉了许久。对于戴奎莫斯·斯科提来说，这片丛林是一片饱含着敌意的陌生土地。有大量经虫蛀的繁茂树木掩住了晨曦，活像是攫取猎食的螯爪，一心阻碍着他们前进。甚至是低矮植物的叶片，也不怀好意地颤动不止。更糟的是，不单是斯科提一人焦虑难安。他的同行者们，这些在卡吉特对格仁诺斯和艾赛的进攻中幸存下来的本地居民，脸上纷纷不加掩饰地流露出恐惧之色。 不独是疯疯癫癫却心地善良的本地生灵，在树丛中尚有其它活物。在他的视野外围，斯科提能看见那些阴影时现，那属于尾随着难民而来的卡吉特，他们在树木上腾跃辗转。当他把脸转向他们时，那些矫健灵活的身影便消失在了黑暗中，仿佛从未存在过一般。可是他知道自己确实亲眼目击了他们。那些波斯莫也看见了，所以加快了步伐。 在十八小时后，被昆虫叮咬着伤口、被密密麻麻的千万道荆棘割伤的他们进入了从一片山谷空地。此时已经入夜，但有一排灼灼燃烧的火炬向着他们迎了过来，照亮了革质帐篷和温蒂斯村遍地随意堆砌的石块。在山谷尽头，火炬之光照耀着一处圣坛，森森树影中，无数枝条彼此相依，拱卫成一座神庙。无需多言，波斯莫们穿过设有火炬的廊道，向着树木走去。斯科提紧随其后。当他们到达这根茎坚固、撑天拄地的活体树木时，仅有一道大门洞开，斯科提能看见昏昧幽暗的蓝光在其中闪烁。百种声调齐鸣而起，汇合成一阵低沉浑厚的哀嚎，在洞中震起无数回音。那位始终带着他前行的波斯莫少女伸出手阻住了他。 “你不会明白的，但没有任何外人得以进入，甚至是朋友也不行。”她说。“这是一座圣地。” 斯科提略一颔首，看着难民们从神庙口低垂着头，鱼贯而入。他们的声音混入了洞中的回响。当最后一个木精灵进入到洞中，斯科提便将注意力转回了村庄。某些地方肯定还储存着食物。缭绕若丝的烟尘和些微烤鹿肉的气息越过火炬的光芒，引着他向气味发出的方向走去。 五名希罗帝尔人、两名布莱顿人和一名诺德人围着一簇营火聚集着。那火将白色的石头烧得发红。他们从一头巨大雄鹿尸体上撕扯下一条条散发着热气的肉片。在斯科提走近他们时，除了一位专心于自己那一大块兽肉的诺德人外，其他人都纷纷站了起来。 “晚上好，很抱歉冒昧打扰，但我想问问能否分我一点吃食。我和格仁诺斯与艾赛的难民们徒步走了整整一天，现在很饿了。” 他们招呼他坐下来用餐，并且各自做了自我介绍。 “看来战争是卷土重来了。”斯科提语气友善地发问道。 “现在这些废物蛋啥都别去惹，就谢天谢地了。”诺德人在咀嚼之余回应道，“我就没瞧见过这么懒散的文化。现在陆上有卡吉特，海上又有高精灵袭击他们。如果有哪个省份活该吃点小苦头，那铁定就是这天杀的威伦森林。” “我可没看见他们有冒犯过你。”布莱顿人中的一位大笑道。 “他们天生就是小贼，简直比卡吉特还坏，连侵略的时候也总是一副乖头乖脑的样子，”诺德人吐出一大块肥肉，那脂肪掉在了被火烤至高温的石头上，“滋滋”一声，发出一阵火焰燃烧油脂的轻响。“他们把自家的森林扩张到不属于他们的领土上，一点点渗透进他们的邻居，艾斯维尔反攻回来他们还摸不着头脑。他们都是些活在最差劲的社会里的混蛋。” “你在这里做什么呢？”斯科提发问。 “我是约赫纳宫廷派来的外交使臣。”诺德人嘀咕着说了一句，又回去吃他的食物。 “那你呢，你是来这干什么的？”其中一个希罗帝尔人问。 “我为帝都的阿特里乌斯大人的建筑委会工作。”斯科提说。“我的一位前同事建议我南下威伦森林。他说战争已经结束，于是我能够借助重修那些在战争中毁于一旦的建筑，为我的公司签下一份满是商机的大合同。灾厄接踵而至，我失去了所有钱财，还跑到了战火重燃的核心地区，而且，我还是没能找到我的前任同事。” “你的前任同事，”另一位自称雷格利乌斯的希罗帝尔人喃喃低语道，“他不会正好就叫利奥德斯·朱鲁斯吧，是他吗？” “你认识他？ “在我混得和你一模一样的时候，他把我引到南部的威伦森林。”雷格利乌斯冷冷地笑道，“我给你老板的竞争对头瓦内奇大人的手下工作，那也是利奥德斯·朱鲁斯曾经工作过的地方。他写信给我，让我代表帝国建筑委会，来签订某种战后重修的合约。我刚刚被解雇，还在想着如果我能带来些新的生意，我就能夺回我的工作。朱鲁斯同我在艾赛会面，并说他要安排一场和希尔维纳的商谈，这其中有大生意可做。” 斯科提诧异不已：“那他现在在哪？” “我不是神学家，所以我也不知道，”雷格利乌斯耸了耸肩。“他死了。在卡吉特们攻打艾赛的时候，他们开始焚烧港口，朱鲁斯那时候正准备着登上他的船离开。或者我应该说，我的船，因为那是花了我的金币买到的。甚至在我们充分意识到发生的事情从而逃离之前，水边的一切便焚作齑粉。卡吉特们可能是群畜生，但是他们很懂得怎么安排进攻。” “我认为他们已经尾随我们穿行过丛林，来到了温蒂斯，”斯科提紧张地陈述着，“在树梢之间肯定有什么生物在成群结队地跳跃。” “可能就是些猴人。”诺德人打了个喷嚏。“没啥好担心的。” “在我们刚到温蒂斯的时候，波斯莫全部进入了那棵树，他们躁动不安，低语着要向敌人释放一种古老的恐怖。”布莱顿人打着战回忆道。“他们就一直站在那，站了足足有一天半了。如果你想找点让你害怕的东西，那就往他们那个方向看一眼。” 另一位布莱顿人，即那位匕落法师公会的代表，在他的同乡发言时，目光茫然地盯着一片黑暗。“可能是吧。但是丛林里也有东西，就在村庄边缘，在向我们窥视。” “可能是其他的难民吧？”斯科提问道，视着让自己保持平静的口吻。 “除非他们现在正穿过树行进，”巫师悄声说着。诺德人和一名希罗帝尔人抓过湿皮革制成的长条油布，将它拉过来盖在了火上，火焰霎时熄灭，连一丝轻微的烧灼声也没有传出。现在斯科提可以看见那些不速之客，它们呈椭圆的黄色眼瞳，以及反射着火光、修长而嗜血的剑刃。他全身冻结在恐惧中，动弹不得，祈祷着自己在它们看来不要太过显眼。 他感到有什么东西碰了碰他的后背，不由倒吸一口凉气。 雷格利乌斯的声音嘶嘶地从上方传来：“看在玛拉的份上，给我安静点，从这爬上来。” 在业已熄灭的营火边缘，从一株高大树木上垂下一根由两道藤蔓绞缠而成的绳子，斯科提一把抓住绳子，全速地向上攀爬，小心地将呼吸放轻，以防有任何喘息声从他口鼻中逃逸出去。在藤蔓的最上端，有一座荒废的巨鸟巢穴坐落在如三叉戟一般的枝杈上，高悬于村庄上空。斯科提刚刚一头栽倒在散发着馥郁气息的柔软麦秆上，就看见雷格利乌斯将藤蔓拉上来收起。下方现已不余一人，而后，在斯科提俯瞰下视时，他也没有看到有任何人影。除了那些朝着那座树木神庙的光辉缓步移动的卡吉特。 “谢谢你，”斯科提耳语道，对这位曾经的竞争者予以他的帮助，内心触动不已。他从村庄方向转过头，看见树木上部的枝柯轻轻触着环抱谷底、生满苔藓的石壁。“你攀爬能力如何？” “你疯了吧，”雷格利乌斯按低了呼吸声说道。“我们就该在这待到他们离开。” “如果他们焚毁了温蒂斯，就像他们对艾赛和格仁诺斯所做的那样，我们下地就死定了。”斯科提开始小心缓慢地尝试爬上树，尝试着每一根枝条是否能供攀登。“你能看见他们在干什么吗？” “说实话，我看不太出来，”雷格利乌斯望向那一片幽暗中。“他们聚集在神庙前方。我想他们也带有……那看起来就像长绳，拖行在他们身后，一直延伸到路上。” 斯科提攀上了一根最为坚固粗硕的枝条，那枝条延伸向潮湿嶙峋的悬崖表面。想跳上崖岸，他所需要跃过的距离并不算远。实际上，这距离近得能让他嗅到丝丝水汽，切身感受到崖石间沁出的阴冷寒意。然而这依旧需要他跳出一步，而在他文员生涯中，他从未做过自去地百尺的树梢间腾跃上陡峭岩石这种事。在他的脑海中，浮现出了那些腾挪于树梢高处、穿林过叶追逐着他的身影。他想起了他们的腿部如何弯曲再发力跳起，他们的手臂如何前伸，优雅灵敏地抓稳树枝。然后，他一步跃起。 他的手努力够向岩石，然而更易于攫获的却是以长条带状生长出的茁茂苔藓。他紧紧抓握住苔藓须茎，然而当他尝试着将双足前蹬时，却不慎失足，两脚朝天。他发现自己上下颠倒，倒悬了几秒，直到他扯着苔藓，将自己拽到一个更习惯的姿势。一道狭窄的露头岩石自峭壁上支起，他得以立足其上，并在落脚后松了口气。 “雷格利乌斯。雷格利乌斯。雷格利乌斯，”斯科提不敢喊出声来。在这一分钟之间，枝杈传来一阵颤动，那位瓦内奇大人的幕僚现身而出。先是背包，再是头，然后是余下的身形全貌。斯科提开始压低嗓子传话，但雷格利乌斯拼命摇头，向下一望。一只卡吉特正盘踞于大树根底，对着营火的余痕虎视眈眈。 雷格利乌斯手脚笨拙地试图在枝头保持平衡，然而尽管枝干强壮坚实，想要仅在空出一只手的情况下保持平衡也万分困难。斯科提将手掌合捧着，对着那背包使了个眼色。对于雷格利乌斯而言，松手放开背包似乎是一个无比痛苦的决定，但他最终妥协了，将包朝着斯科提投掷了过去。 在背包上，有一处微不可见的破口，而当斯科提接住了背包的时候，一枚金币从包里掉了出来。它在下坠过程中与石壁磕碰弹起，叩击出清脆的声响，这声音高亢又轻柔，仿佛是斯科提有生以来听到的最为刺耳的警报声。 一切发生得太快。 树下的卡塞-拉特目光上巡，猛然怒号一声。随着那只下方的猫俯身一跃，攀上了低矮处的树枝，余下的卡吉特纷纷啸聚而应。雷格利乌斯看见它从自己下方以难以置信的灵敏向上攀跃，顿时陷入了恐惧。甚至在他起跳之前，斯科提也能判断出他将会坠落下去。伴随着一声尖叫，文员雷格利乌斯猛然下坠，颈部受冲力一挫，立刻折断。 一道白色光焰骤然从遍布神庙的裂隙间喷涌而出，而波斯莫祈祷者们的呻吟开始转变为某种仿佛来自异界的可怖声响。那只向上攀爬的卡塞-拉特停止了爬行，凝望着神庙。 “凯尔苟啊，”它倒吸一口凉气。“是狂猎。” 似乎在现实中被劈开一条缝隙。大群生着可怖面貌的异兽浩荡奔涌而出，蟾蜍遍体触须，昆虫披甲带刺，巨蛇长躯似胶，更有那聚气为形的神头鬼脸之辈，暴怒难遏，自巨大圣树间倾巢而出。它们撕碎了神庙前的卡吉特。余下的猫纷纷向丛林窜逃，然而在它们逃亡的同时，也开始拉拽起各自携带着的绳索。数秒之后，整座温蒂斯村就被一群癫狂错乱的狂猎鬼怪搅得喧嚣如沸。 一片嘈杂吼啸与群声嚣叫中，斯科提听着藏身的希罗帝尔人在被吞食时惨嚎出声。诺德人同样被搜捉出来果腹，那些布莱顿人也遭遇了同样的境遇。尽管巫师施法将自己隐形，然而兽群并不依赖视觉掠食。那株卡塞-拉特容身的树木被一股极大的巨力所摧折，开始剧烈摇曳晃动起来。斯科提看见了卡吉特被恐惧侵袭的双眼，随后递出了一根苔藓编连的长条。 当那只猫跳向藤蔓时，它的面孔上显露出令人哀悯的感激之意。斯科提往回拉扯着藤条时，它脸上还保持着那副感激神态，接着，斯科提就看见它跌落了下去。在它触地之前，狂猎就将他一身血肉吞噬殆尽，只留下骨架一具。 斯科提自己尝试跳向下一处耸峙而出的露岩，而这一次则要成功得多。在那里，他竭力拖着身体爬到了悬崖巅峰，于此处便可俯瞰那片由温蒂斯村化作的暴乱。一众狂猎数量愈增，逐渐自出峡谷的道路涌出，追猎逃亡的卡吉特。那时才是疯嚣肆虐之始。 借着月色，从斯科提居高临下的位置看去，他得以观察到卡吉特在何处缠缚着它们的绳索。随着一声轰鸣震响如雷，宛如雪崩一般的坍塌自山体爆发，挟着滚滚山石倾泻而下，阻绝了道路。待烟尘肃清，他才望见整座村庄已被坍塌的巨石封锁了出路，失去捕杀猎物的出路，狂猎只能开始自相残杀。 斯科提偏过头来，不忍再目睹这同类相食的狂欢。夜色中的丛林矗立其后，一片密林交织成网。他一把将雷格利乌斯的背包挂在肩上，走了进去。

english:
[pagebreak]

SITHIS

ithis is the start of the house. Before him was nothing, but the foolish Altmer have names for and revere this nothing. That is because they are lazy slaves. Indeed, from the Sermons, 'stasis asks merely for itself, which is nothing.' Sithis sundered the nothing and mutated the parts, fashioning from them a myriad of possibilities. These ideas ebbed and flowed and faded away and this is how it should have been. One idea, however, became jealous and did not want to die; like the stasis, he wanted to last. This was the demon Anui-El, who made friends, and they called themselves the Aedra. They enslaved everything that Sithis had made and created realms of everlasting imperfection. Thus are the Aedra the false gods, that is, illusion. So Sithis begat Lorkhan and sent him to destroy the universe. Lorkhan! Unstable mutant! Lorkhan had found the Aedric weakness. While each rebel was, by their nature, immeasurable, they were, through jealously and vanity, also separate from each other. They were also unwilling to go back to the nothing of before. So while they ruled their false dominions, Lorkhan filled the void with a myriad of new ideas. These ideas were legion. Soon it seemed that Lorkhan had a dominion of his own, with slaves and everlasting imperfections, and he seemed, for all the world, like an Aedra. Thus did he present himself as such to the demon Anui-El and the Eight Givers: as a friend. Go unto the Sharmat Dagoth Ur as a friend. AE HERMA MORA ALTADOON PADHOME LKHAN AE AI.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

西帝斯

西帝斯是同族之始，在他之前是“无”。但愚蠢的傲特莫为这虚无命名并加以崇敬。只不过因为他们是怠惰的奴隶罢了。事实上，三十六课有云，“‘静’所追求的仅仅是它自身，也就是一切为空。” 西帝斯将这“无”裂解，使其部分变换，在其中塑造出无数可能性。这些想法涨落如潮，逐渐消散，这才是其当有的模样。 然而有一个想法感到嫉妒且不想死，和“静”一样，他想要延续。恶魔阿努-艾尔，他广结好友然后自称伊德拉。他们奴役了西帝斯创造的一切，创造了永不完美的领域。因此伊德拉是伪神，也就是幻象。 所以西帝斯创造了洛克汗，并指派他去毁灭宇宙。洛克汗！不稳定的突变！ 洛克汗发现了伊德拉性的弱点。虽然每个叛军的本性都不可估量，但出于嫉妒与虚荣，他们是彼此独立的。同时他们也不愿意回归之前的“无”。所以当他们统治着自己虚妄的领土时，洛克汗用新想法填充了虚空。想法之多不可胜数。很快洛克汗似乎拥有了属于自己的统治权，拥有奴隶和永不完美的造物，对于全世界来说，他看起来就像是一位伊德拉。因此他以这样的身份出现在恶魔阿努-艾尔和八位给予者面前：朋友。 你须以朋友身份去见萨玛特·达格斯·乌尔。 AE HERMA MORA ALTADOON PADHOME LKHAN AE AI.

english:
[pagebreak]

The Red Book of Riddles

[pagebreak]

his handye booke doth containe alle diverse manner of riddles and follyes, and, by means of carefulle studye, the prudente scholarlye gentlemane maye finde himselfe noe longer discomfited by the sharpe wite of his fellowes. The posing and puzzling of riddles is a convention of polite aristocratic Western society. Nobles and social aspirants collect books of riddles and study them, hoping thereby to increase the chances of their appearing sly and witty in conversation. The question: It has a tail, a side and a head I call it what I call a snake It has no body and it is dead The answer: It must be a drake The question: Poets know the hearts of Men and Mer But beasts can't know my heart, you see This book was written by a bear The answer: It is not a book of poetry. The question: I gave you a sock, not unlike a box With hammers and nails all around it Two lids open when it knocks The answer: It must have been a great hit.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

红色谜语书

[pagebreak]

本书包含各种谜语和俏皮话，通过仔细研究，谨慎的学者和绅士将不会再被同伴的机智所迷惑。 西方彬彬有礼的贵族社会素来讲求腔调和话术。贵族和有社会报复的人会收集并研究谜语书籍，希望凭此增添他们在谈话中展露狡猾和机智的机会。 问题： 什么东西有头有尾还有侧面 我说的发音像蛇 没有身子也已经死了。 答案： 这一定是龙。 问题： 诗人了解人与莫的心， 但你明白野兽不可能知道我的心， 这本书是熊写的。 答案： 这肯定不是一本诗集。 问题： 我给了你一棒，而不是简单的一拳， 棒上全是锤头和钉子， 一棒下去俩大口子。 答案： 这肯定是很重的一击。
englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

the red book of riddles

[pagebreak]

his handye booke doth containe alle diverse manner of riddles and follyes, and, by means of carefulle studye, the prudente scholarlye gentlemane maye finde himselfe noe longer discomfited by the sharpe wite of his fellowes. the posing and puzzling of riddles is a convention of polite aristocratic western society. nobles and social aspirants collect books of riddles and study them, hoping thereby to increase the chances of their appearing sly and witty in conversation. the question: it has a tail, a side and a head i call it what i call a snake it has no body and it is dead the answer: it must be a drake the question: poets know the hearts of men and mer but beasts can't know my heart, you see this book was written by a bear the answer: it is not a book of poetry. the question: i gave you a sock, not unlike a box with hammers and nails all around it two lids open when it knocks the answer: it must have been a great hit.

红色谜语书

[pagebreak]

本书包含各种谜语和俏皮话，通过仔细研究，谨慎的学者和绅士将不会再被同伴的机智所迷惑。 西方彬彬有礼的贵族社会素来讲求腔调和话术。贵族和有社会报复的人会收集并研究谜语书籍，希望凭此增添他们在谈话中展露狡猾和机智的机会。 问题： 什么东西有头有尾还有侧面 我说的发音像蛇 没有身子也已经死了。 答案： 这一定是龙。 问题： 诗人了解人与莫的心， 但你明白野兽不可能知道我的心， 这本书是熊写的。 答案： 这肯定不是一本诗集。 问题： 我给了你一棒，而不是简单的一拳， 棒上全是锤头和钉子， 一棒下去俩大口子。 答案： 这肯定是很重的一击。
agentText:
Addon: Skyrim EDID: Book4RareRedBookofRiddles Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 009630 English: [pagebreak]

The Red Book of Riddles

[pagebreak]

his handye booke doth containe alle diverse manner of riddles and follyes, and, by means of carefulle studye, the prudente scholarlye gentlemane maye finde himselfe noe longer discomfited by the sharpe wite of his fellowes. The posing and puzzling of riddles is a convention of polite aristocratic Western society. Nobles and social aspirants collect books of riddles and study them, hoping thereby to increase the chances of their appearing sly and witty in conversation. The question: It has a tail, a side and a head I call it what I call a snake It has no body and it is dead The answer: It must be a drake The question: Poets know the hearts of Men and Mer But beasts can't know my heart, you see This book was written by a bear The answer: It is not a book of poetry. The question: I gave you a sock, not unlike a box With hammers and nails all around it Two lids open when it knocks The answer: It must have been a great hit. Chinese: [pagebreak]

english: <font face='$HandwrittenFont' size='20'> Lucky to have talked my way out of the gates at Bruma. Time to leave Cyrodiil. Skyrim, not so bad if you don't mind the smell of mammoth. Maybe I can forget the research, forget the contract. The air is clean here. Caravan brought questions with it. They'll catch up sooner or later. Money won't do any good. Need some dwarven junk to pacify them, but how? Markarth was promising, but locked up tight and I'm no thief. Need to find another way. Highwaymen killed the driver and tried to rob me. Vivec must have been with me, managed to talk them into working for me to excavate dwarven ruins they are camped in. Safer than the cities anyway. These bandits are thick, but nobody's going to find me down here. Actually found some artifacts, must try to find a buyer. Maybe I'll actually make enough coin to pay these fools what I've promised for their labor. Can't go to Markarth again. Staying in the ruins for now. What's my next move?
chinese: <font face='$HandwrittenFont' size='20'> 万幸我在布鲁玛找到了出路。是时候离开希罗帝尔了。 如果不介意猛犸的味道，天际也不算很糟。也许我可以忘掉研究、忘掉合同。这里的空气很干净。 商队带来了问题。他们迟早会赶上来。给钱是没用了。得用些矮人垃圾来安抚他们，但怎么办呢？ 马卡斯倒是很有前途，但被管得严严实实，我又不是个贼。得另找地方了。 强盗杀了车夫还想抢劫我。维威克一定与我同在，我说服了他们为我工作，去挖掘他们驻扎的矮人遗迹。反正比城里安全点。 这些强盗很厉害，但没人能找到我。我居然发现了一些文物，一定要努力找买家。也许我能赚到足够的钱来给这些傻瓜付报酬。 不能再去马卡斯了。暂时待在遗迹里。我下一步该怎么做？

agentText:
Addon: Skyrim
EDID: dunMzinchaleftMalurilsJournal
Record: BOOK:DESC
SID: 00972C
English: <font face='$HandwrittenFont' size='20'> Lucky to have talked my way out of the gates at Bruma. Time to leave Cyrodiil. Skyrim, not so bad if you don't mind the smell of mammoth. Maybe I can forget the research, forget the contract. The air is clean here. Caravan brought questions with it. They'll catch up sooner or later. Money won't do any good. Need some dwarven junk to pacify them, but how? Markarth was promising, but locked up tight and I'm no thief. Need to find another way. Highwaymen killed the driver and tried to rob me.
